seoTitle: Create and Track Email Campaigns in HubSpot
metaDescription: Design a HubSpot email campaign and monitor its performance, from setting campaign tags to reviewing open rates, clicks, and bounce data.
intro: Creating a HubSpot email campaign involves more than just sending a message — it means tagging the campaign so you can measure its performance afterward. This guide covers building the email with the drag-and-drop editor, setting subject lines and recipient lists, and then reviewing analytics once it's live. It ends with matching engagement data back to individual CRM contact records.

steps.heading: Open the Email dashboard
steps.body: Go to Marketing in HubSpot and click into the Email directory.

steps.heading: Start a new campaign
steps.body: Click Create Email and set your marketing campaign parameters and tags.

steps.heading: Choose a template and format content
steps.body: Select a baseline campaign template and arrange content inside the drag-and-drop grid.

steps.heading: Set your subject line and recipients
steps.body: Write an engaging subject line, select your recipient lists, and finalize your sender setup.

steps.heading: Send or schedule
steps.body: Send immediately or schedule delivery for a peak engagement window.

steps.heading: Review performance
steps.body: Open the Analyze tab to review open rates, click tracking, and export custom reports.

faq.question: How do I track how well my HubSpot email campaign performed?
faq.answer: The Analyze tab shows real-time metrics like open rates, click tracking, and bounce data after the campaign sends.

faq.question: Can I export campaign performance data?
faq.answer: Yes, you can export custom reports from the Analyze tab for sharing with stakeholders.

faq.question: Does HubSpot tie email engagement back to contact records?
faq.answer: Yes, tracking codes match email engagement with the corresponding CRM contact record.

faq.question: Should I schedule my campaign or send it immediately?
faq.answer: That depends on your audience, but scheduling for a peak engagement window can improve open rates.
